models_tested.notes: Most rhetorical development per lane; strongest dissent preservation. Missed the foundational arithmetic issue. Self-assessed as strongest before comparison; comparison revealed overconfidence.
Lowest token cost. Cited xAI provider docs directly. One citation requires verification (getmaxim.ai claim). Missed arithmetic and meta-observation.
Caught arithmetic in BRIEF. Unique technical distinction between prompt-prefix caching and semantic-response caching. Highest citation source quality. Explicit fourth path.

Caught arithmetic AND proposed reconciliation hypothesis. Named moat-commoditization clearly. Operationally specific Path D. Highest information density. Some citations from non-institutional sources.

headline_findings.finding: Foundational defect in v1.0.0 BRIEF section
headline_findings.evidence: 2 of 4 frontier models missed arithmetic inconsistency that v1.0.0 prompt did not require validating

headline_findings.finding: Verdict_action variance is correct behavior on contested directives
headline_findings.evidence: 3 of 4 runs converged on REVISIT; Claude landed at CONTESTED. Both readings are defensible.

headline_findings.finding: Output token count does not correlate with audit quality
headline_findings.evidence: Gemini ~1,500 tokens caught more issues than Claude ~5,500 tokens
headline_findings.remediation: Eval framework should weight information density, not length

headline_findings.finding: Citation source quality varies meaningfully across models
headline_findings.evidence: GPT/Claude leaned institutional; Grok/Gemini leaned mixed
headline_findings.remediation: Eval framework should record citation source tier as separate signal

headline_findings.finding: Cost spread is ~22x across validated frontier models
headline_findings.remediation: Recommendation hierarchy should surface this trade-off explicitly

open_questions_post_eval: Quality cliff: do mid-tier models (Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4 mini, Gemini 2.5 Flash) execute consensus_protocol acceptably?
Cross-directive stability: how does the prompt perform on directive types beyond financial/strategic decisions?
v1.1.0 regression test: does the new version achieve 100% catch rate on the arithmetic check?
